verdict_reason: The disagreement on 'tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni' surfaces the substantive doctrinal axis: whether the universalising scope is metaphor or literal. Shankara reads the tasmāt as pulling the conclusion forward to all beings; Ramanuja reads sarvāṇi bhūtāni as literal universalism (gods to immovables). The English translators preserve the universal scope but lose the rhetorical force of tasmāt closing a 20-verse argument; the explanation names this. Doctrinally substantive, not editorial.
